previousAttemptIssues.attempt1Rejection: cms.contentitemmemberrole was incorrectly added to the Binding object types table. ContentItemMemberRoleInfo has ContinuousIntegrationSettings.Enabled = false — it does not have its own CI folder and is serialized into content item XML via ContentItemMemberRoleCustomProcessor.
previousAttemptIssues.attempt2Resolution: The binding table row has been removed. The General section note at line 111 correctly documents the serialization behavior. Verified.

claims.claim: cms.memberrole is a CI/CD-supported object type with its own CI folder
claims.location: General section, row at lines 101-113, CI/CD folder name cell (line 107)
claims.evidence: MemberRoleInfo.cs line 22: OBJECT_TYPE = "cms.memberrole"; lines 31-34: ContinuousIntegrationSettings = { Enabled = true }. CI folder name derives from OBJECT_TYPE, so folder is cms.memberrole.
claims.docFact: AREA-015: ContentItemMemberRoleCustomProcessor and CI filter already existed on master. No changes in this commit, but types are CI/CD supported.

claims.claim: Member roles are used to control content access for registered members
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings (AREA-002) implements three-tier model: Public, Secured (any auth), SecuredWithRoles (specific member roles). MemberRoleInfo stores role definitions. ContentItemMemberRoleInfo binds roles to content items. Code-analysis AREA-002 confirms: 'Three-tier content access model: Public (no auth), Secured (any authenticated user), SecuredWithRoles (specific member roles)'.

claims.claim: Member role assignments to content items use the object type cms.contentitemmemberrole
claims.location: General section, Notes cell (line 111), inline code reference
claims.evidence: ContentItemMemberRoleInfo.cs line 23: OBJECT_TYPE = "cms.contentitemmemberrole". Line 35: IsBinding = true. DependsOn includes MemberRoleInfo.OBJECT_TYPE with ObjectDependencyEnum.Binding.

claims.claim: cms.contentitemmemberrole data is serialized as part of content item data (not its own CI folder)
claims.evidence: ContentItemMemberRoleInfo.cs lines 36-40: ContinuousIntegrationSettings = { Enabled = false, UsesCustomFiltering = true }. ContentItemMemberRoleCustomProcessor.cs line 127-138: PostprocessSerializedObject() takes a ContentItemInfo, queries its member role bindings, and stores them into the content item's XML via StoreContentItemMemberRolesToXml(). Line 43: PostprocessDeserializedDocument() deserializes them back. This confirms member role bindings are embedded in content item CI files, not in their own folder.
claims.docFact: AREA-015: ContentItemMemberRoleCustomProcessor and CI filter already existed on master.

claims.claim: The inpage_link cicd_contentitems correctly points to the Content items row in the same page
claims.evidence: Line 336 of the same file: {% anchor cicd_contentitems %}{% page_link barWCQ linkText="Content items" %}. The anchor is defined in the Content items row of the General table.

claims.claim: The page_link member_roles_xp resolves to a valid Member roles page
claims.evidence: src/_documentation/_documentation/developers-and-admins/development/registration-and-authentication/member-roles.md has front matter: identifier: member_roles_xp, with redirect_from: x/member_roles_xp.

claims.claim: cms.contentitemmemberrole is NOT listed in the Binding object types table
claims.evidence: Searched entire Binding section (lines 1189-1459). The only occurrence of 'contentitemmemberrole' in the file is at line 111 in the General section note. The Binding table contains: cms.allowedchildcontenttype, cms.applicationpermission, cms.workflowtransition, cms.contenttypechannel, emaillibrary.emailtemplatecontenttype, emaillibrary.sendconfigurationcontactgroup, cms.macrorulemacrorulecategory, commerce.orderstatusnotification, cms.userrole, cms.webpagescopecontenttype, cms.contentworkflowcontenttype, cms.contentworkflowrole, cms.contentworkflowsteprole, cms.workspacedatapermission. No member role bindings present. This is correct because ContentItemMemberRoleInfo has ContinuousIntegrationSettings.Enabled = false.

claims.claim: cms.memberrolemember is correctly absent from all tables (not CI/CD supported as standalone)
claims.evidence: MemberRoleMemberInfo.cs lines 35-38: ContinuousIntegrationSettings = { Enabled = false }. No UsesCustomFiltering is set. This type has no CI/CD support and no custom processor. Correctly excluded from all tables.

analogousTypes.note: Confirmed Enabled = false. Not in Binding table. Consistent.

Confirmed Enabled = false, UsesCustomFiltering = true. Not in Binding table. Consistent.

crossPatternValidation.verdict: The cms.contentitemmemberrole treatment (absent from Binding table, documented via note in General section) is consistent with how all other embedded content item binding types are handled.
